title: Samsung teases the Galaxy S26 Ultra's nighttime photography chops
description: Samsung is finally unveiling the Galaxy S26, S26+, and S26 Ultra on February 25 (missing the opportunity to align with the names and the year and have it take place on February 26). Ahead of that, the company is now in full teaser mode. Today, Samsung's US arm posted the video teaser you can see below to its X account. No spotlight needed. Can your phone do that? 🔆 Get ready for the next level of Nightography with #GalaxyAI to light up any occasion.Reserve now: https://t.co/dTlilFNoHy pic.twitter.com/hTRTenNLjL— Samsung Mobile US (@SamsungMobileUS) February 12, 2026 The teaser is...

title: Honor Pad X8b quietly gets official and is already available to buy
description: The Honor Pad X8 was announced in 2022, and now the Honor Pad X8b has quietly been launched in Saudi Arabia. We won't even try to make sense of the naming scheme and why, three and a half years after the X8, the X8b has arrived. The new tablet has an 11-inch 1920x1200 IPS LCD screen with a 90Hz refresh rate, a 5MP rear camera, a 5MP front camera, and a 10,100 mAh battery. The Honor Pad X8b is powered by the Snapdragon 680 SoC, paired with 4GB of RAM and 128GB of storage. It runs Android 16 with MagicOS 10 on top, it's 7.25mm thin, and weighs 496g. It comes with a charger in the box, at...

title: Huawei Band 11 Pro leaks ahead of launch
description: Huawei is gearing up to hold a major launch event later this month and we’ve now gotten our first look at one of the new wearables that’s set to make its debut. The Huawei Band 11 Pro appeared inside the Huawei Smart Life app, giving us our first look at the device. We can see it will offer a refined look compared to the Band 10 with a curved screen and a metal casing with rounded corners. Huawei Band 11 Pro We can see two color options, with one featuring a bright green braided strap and another sporting a silicone strap in a more toned-down light blue option. There are no...

title: Sony LinkBuds Clip unboxing
description: Sony was an early entrant into the open wear stereo (OWS) earbuds market with its original LinkBuds back in 2022 and its latest addition takes a different stylistic approach. The LinkBuds Clip arrive on the scene with the trendy clip-on design alongside claims of refined audio quality and up to 37 hours of continuous usage between the bud and its case. Let’s take a closer look at what comes with the LinkBuds Clip. They are packaged in the typical recycled cardboard box that you get with all recent Sony headphones and earbuds. You’re greeted by the charging/carrying case, which...

title: Realme P4 Power review
The AI-powered Siri is still on track for 2026
description: Apple is obviously facing significant hurdles with the development of its own AI-powered Apple Intelligence and the upgraded virtual assistant Siri. The company first announced Apple Intelligence during WWDC 2024, but failed to deliver. On top of that, Mark Gurman reported that the upgraded Siri won't make it into the iOS 26.4 update later this year, due to issues that came out during internal testing. However, that story was based on an internal report by one of Apple's employees. A company executive was asked about this by CNBC and the answer was simple yet reassuring: "Still on...

title: The Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones get a new color
description: Sony has introduced a new color for its flagship wireless WH-1000XM6 headphones. Not to be confused with the recently launched WF-1000XM6, which are the in-ear model. The WH-1000XM6 will now be available in Sand Pink. This is a very light, pastel shade of pink that really challenges the notion of what pink really is. You get the same soft-touch matte texture all over with a fine speckled finish when viewed up close. Completing the look are rose gold highlights around the hinges. The Sand Pink is the fourth color option for these headphones, which were previously...

title: Samsung Galaxy A37 and A57 are now listed in the Google Play Console
description: Samsung's Galaxy A37 and Galaxy A57 are now listed in the Google Play Console, a sure sign that their official launch is very near. The listings confirm that the Galaxy A37 is powered by Samsung's Exynos 1480 SoC, paired with 6GB of RAM in one variant (though more will undoubtedly be offered). The phone will run Android 16 out of the box, unsurprisingly. Here it is in the official image revealed by the listing. The Galaxy A57 is powered by the Exynos 1680 SoC, paired with 8GB of RAM in one version. This one runs Android 16 too. A past Geekbench run revealed that it would have a 12GB...

title: iPhone 17e design revealed in new video ahead of expected launch
description: Apple’s new affordable iPhone is rumored to launch later this month, with one leak pointing to a February 19 announcement. While nothing has been confirmed, YouTuber Jon Prosser has shared what appears to be the first look at the upcoming iPhone 17e. The video shared by the YouTuber showcases the iPhone 17e with a Dynamic Island and MagSafe charging support. However, most recent leaks and reports have suggested that Apple won’t be including the Dynamic Island on the upcoming iPhone. Instead, it’s likely to stick to the notch present on the iPhone 16e. In terms of design, the...

title: Xiaomi 18 render leaks, shows an iPhone 16 Pro lookalike
description: Chinese smartphone makers' obsession with Apple and 'emulating' the Cupertino company's designs shows no sign of stopping. Today a new render leaked in China, purportedly showing us the Xiaomi 18, which is only due to be unveiled in September. There's still a very long time to go until then, which is why you should take this with a grain of salt, but anyway, here it is: the iPhone 16 Pro in all its glory. Sorry, we meant the Xiaomi 18. Take a look: The comparison with Apple's device from 2024 is pretty much inevitable. The design of the camera island is very close, even though...

title: Motorola to launch two more FIFA-themed smartphones
description: Motorola unveiled the Razr 60 FIFA World Cup 26 Edition last month, which went on sale in the US yesterday. We now hear that Motorola plans to launch two more FIFA-themed smartphones. The word comes from leakster Evan Blass, who said that a FIFA-themed Razr Fold and Signature are also expected from Motorola, without revealing their launch timeframe. Motorola Razr 60 FIFA World Cup 26 Edition The Motorola Razr 60 FIFA World Cup 26 Edition is basically the Razr 60 featuring the FIFA World Cup 2026 logo on its back panel, and it comes with a "curated collection of exclusive FIFA World...

title: Nothing Phone (4a) and (4a) Pro storage and color options leaked
description: Nothing recently began teasing its upcoming Phone (4a) series, which is expected to launch as early as next month. While details about the devices remain scarce, CEO Carl Pei has confirmed that the (4a) lineup will bring major improvements over the Phone (3a) series. A tipster has now revealed the storage and color options for the Nothing Phone (4a) and Phone (4a) Pro. Nothing Phone (3a) and Phone (3a) Pro The Phone (4a) and Phone (4a) Pro are tipped to arrive in 8GB/128GB and 12GB/256GB configurations. Nothing has also confirmed that the upcoming models will use UFS 3.1 storage,...

title: Tecno Pova Curve 2 debuts with 8,000mAh battery, 6.78" curved display
description: The Tecno Pova Curve 2 has been launched in India. It comes with a curved display and is claimed to be the slimmest phone with an 8,000mAh battery. It also gets a refreshed design over the original Pova Curve. At the front, the Pova Curve 2 gets a 6.78-inch curved AMOLED display that offers a 1.5K resolution and a refresh rate of 144Hz. The panel offers up to 4,500 nits of peak brightness as well. The display is protected by Corning’s Gorilla Glass 7i, whereas the phone offers an IP64 rating as well as SGS certified drop protection. You’ll find a dual camera setup at the back...

title: Nothing Phone (4a) runs Geekbench, reveals the chipset powering it
description: The Nothing Phone (4a) has run the Geekbench AI benchmark today. The phone has the model number A069, runs Android 16, and the prototype running the benchmark has 12GB of RAM, though we assume there will be lower amounts of memory offered as well. The benchmark run has revealed that the Nothing Phone (4a) is powered by the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 SoC. According to previous rumors, the Phone (4a) will launch alongside the Phone (4a) Pro in early March. At least one of them will use UFS 3.1 storage, up from UFS 2.2 on the Nothing Phone (3a) and Nothing Phone (3a) Pro. The new models will...

title: Google Pixel 10a leaks in dozens of official-looking images
description: The fact that it might actually be nothing more than a re-released Pixel 9a hasn't stopped Google's upcoming Pixel 10a from leaking like crazy in the past few weeks, ahead of its full unveiling that's taking place on February 18. Today we've already seen the Pixel 10a's EU Energy Label revealing its IP68 dust and water resistance rating, A rating for drops, B rating for repairability, and the fact that its battery will charge for 1,000 cycles before losing more than 20% of its initial capacity. Now, we have more leaked images, showing the phone in all colorways from a lot of...

title: Infinix Note 60 Ultra runs Geekbench too, here's the chipset at the helm
description: The Infinix Note 60 was already spotted in the Geekbench online database, and today the Note 60 Ultra ran the benchmark too, with the model number X6877. As you can see, it managed a single-core score of 1,609 and a multi-core score of 6,762 in Geekbench 6.6. The Note 60 Ultra is powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 8400 SoC, which is a bit of a shame since the Dimensity 8500 was made official recently. Anyway, this was paired with 12GB of RAM in the prototype which ran the benchmark. The phone will run Android 16 from day one, with Infinix's XOS 16 on top. It's designed by...

title: Poco X8 Pro Max gets certified ahead of its release
description: The Poco X8 Pro got certified last month in Thailand, and now the X8 Pro Max has been certified in Indonesia by TKDN. The Poco X8 Pro Max has the model number 2602BPC18G, which was recently tied to the X8 Pro Max name by an IMEI database listing. The Poco X8 Pro Max was also certified by IMDA in Singapore and the EEC in the past. The Indonesian certification doesn't reveal any of its specs, but a leak in late January, based on information from code in Xiaomi's HyperOS 3, told us that the X8 Pro Max will have an 8,500 mAh battery, while the Poco X8 Pro will go with 'just' 6,500 mAh. Both...

title: Xiaomi Watch 5 EU pricing leaks
description: The Xiaomi Watch 5 launched in China in late December, and now it's headed to international markets, including the EU, according to a new report from Czechia. The global version of the watch will run Wear OS, and the price in Czechia will allegedly be CZK 7,990, which right now translates into approximately €329. The watch will apparently be offered in Juniper Green and black. While this price applies to the model without data connectivity, a Polish comparison site suggests the version with eSIM is also headed to the EU. Xiaomi Watch 5 The Xiaomi Watch 5 has an insanely big...

title: Here's when the Infinix Note Edge is launching in India
description: Infinix made the Note Edge official last month, and now we know when it will finally launch in India: on February 18 at 12 PM local time. This information comes courtesy of a Flipkart microsite that went live for the device. This obviously also tells us that the Note Edge will be sold in India by Flipkart. The microsite reveals that Flipkart will be offering the device in Lunar Titanium, Stellar Blue, and Silk Green. The Note Edge will be India's first smartphone equipped with the MediaTek Dimensity 7100 SoC. It has a 6.78-inch 1208x2644 AMOLED screen with 120Hz refresh rate and...

title: Motorola Razr FIFA World Cup 26 Edition is now available
description: Motorola unveiled the Razr 60 FIFA World Cup 26 Edition smartphone in early January, and today it's become available to purchase from the company's official website. [#InlinePriceWidget,13749,1#] It will set you back $699.99. It comes unlocked, with 256GB of storage. Spec-wise, it's identical to any other Razr 60, but it does come with a special colorway inspired by the game, featuring gold tone Motorola and FIFA World Cup 26 logos on the back. You also get exclusive wallpapers and a ringtone of the official tournament jingle. The phone comes with a 6.9-inch LTPO AMOLED folding...
